---
title: "Nick Anderson"
output: html_document
theme: null
---

# **Welcome, here are some projects of mine**

My main project is a three-part institutional and intellectual history of The New School's economics department. A 121-page draft of Part 1 is available here:

[**Economics at The New School — 2024 draft (Google Drive)**](https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/17Ip5x5DqTrQo8c7PagMYfp7mKX2KJDvg?usp=sharing)

---

Here are some other projects and pages on this site:

1. [**TNSE — Economics at The New School**](tnse.html)
A three-part institutional and intellectual history of The New School's economics department from its 1919 founding to the present. Part 1 (121 pages drafted) covers 1919–1933. Parts 2 and 3 extend the history to 1960 and include an oral history component with 84 structured interviews.

2. [**Research**](research.html)
Empirical work on public finance, tax revenue cyclicality, historical balance-of-payments data, and a replication and extension of the empirical data in Anwar Shaikh's *Capitalism* (2016). Includes original figures built from U.S. Treasury and BEA data.

3. [**realecon.org**](https://realecon.org)
A public website built as a companion to Anwar Shaikh's work in economics. Hosts author bibliographies, forum discussions, and resources for heterodox and classical political economy.

4. [**HMDA Explore**](https://andenick.shinyapps.io/HMDAExplore/)
An interactive 33-year Home Mortgage Disclosure Act explorer built during my Schwartz Center research assistantship at The New School. Please allow the application a moment to spin up.

5. [**AI Tools**](aitools.html)
Open-source research infrastructure for AI-assisted empirical economics. Protocols for PDF processing, reproducible economic data construction, and pipeline architecture for empirical research.

6. [**Projects**](projects.html)
Public code repositories and data tools, including FreeNIC (banking regulatory data 1863–2026), Foodberg (historical food prices), and a Westchester County sidewalk-accessibility analysis.

---

Thank you and let me know if you have any questions!

Nick

---

Last compiled: `r Sys.Date()`

<style type="text/css">
.title {
  display: none;
}

.navbar-default {
    background-color: #640000 !important;
}

.navbar .navbar-brand, .navbar .navbar-nav .nav-links  {
  color: white;
}

.navbar-default .navbar-nav>li>a {
    color: white !important;
    font-weight: 150;
}

.navbar-nav > .active > a {
    background-color: #006400 !important;
    font-weight: 175;
}
</style>
